extracted_fields.client_problem: ABC's operations are heavily manual — staff spend hours preparing reports, following up on approvals, checking inventory manually, and consolidating data across multiple systems. As the business scales this is becoming unmanageable and consuming bandwidth that should go toward decision-making.
strict_requirements: AI-agent-driven operational platform (not just a dashboard)
Reporting agent: auto-collect data, generate summaries, send daily reports
Inventory agent: monitor patterns, proactively alert on stock shortages
Human-in-the-loop approvals for all AI decisions
extracted_fields.timeline: 14–16 weeks total: Discovery & AI agent design (2 weeks), Core platform development (6–8 weeks), AI agent training & integration (4 weeks), Testing & deployment (2 weeks)
extracted_fields.budget: $60,000 – $85,000 for MVP depending on integrations, AI complexity, data readiness, and scale requirements
next_steps: Disha to prepare architecture proposal and AI agent workflow designs
Disha to prepare implementation roadmap and commercials
Share full proposal with Akshat's team within next couple of days
Akshat to review proposal and align with internal stakeholders
Identify all decision-makers and stakeholders on the ABC side
Schedule follow-up call to discuss proposal and agree on engagement
extracted_fields.call_summary: A solid discovery and solution-alignment call where the client's pain around manual operations was clearly surfaced and a well-structured AI-agent platform was proposed. The call lost momentum mid-way due to a 40-second audio issue, and the closing lacked a confirmed follow-up date and stakeholder identification.
call_highlights: Client pain point was drawn out naturally before any solution was presented
Reliability objection handled confidently with human-in-the-loop framing
Concrete agent examples (reporting, inventory) made the value proposition tangible
call_concerns: Audio breakdown at 07:04–07:47 disrupted flow and lost client engagement momentum
Decision-makers on the client side were never identified beyond Akshat
Next steps lacked a specific follow-up date and named owner on the client side
extracted_fields.individual_summary: Disha demonstrated strong discovery skills, letting the client fully articulate the problem before pitching, and handled the reliability objection effectively. Presentation of the tech stack and phased timeline was clear and credible.
individual_strengths: Active listening — reflected client problems back accurately before proposing solutions
Objection handling — reframed AI agents as intelligent assistants with human oversight
individual_improvements: Should have probed for other decision-makers and stakeholders early in the call
Closing needed a specific date for the follow-up, not just 'next couple of days'
call_insights: Client is early in their AI journey — education-first approach worked well
Scalability concern was implied throughout; worth leading with it in the proposal
Budget range of $60K–$85K was introduced by the sales rep without pushback — client did not negotiate
Audio quality issues were a significant risk to deal momentum and should be resolved before the next call
extracted_fields.conclusions: The call established strong alignment on the problem and proposed solution. The deal has real momentum — the client showed consistent enthusiasm and no hard objections. The next critical step is delivering a compelling proposal within the promised timeframe to maintain that momentum.

speaker_scores.name: Disha
speaker_scores.summary: Led the call effectively with good discovery and a clear solution pitch, though the closing needed a firmer commitment on next steps.
strengths: Strong active listening and problem reflection
Handled reliability objection with human-in-the-loop framing
improvements: Confirm specific follow-up date at close

speaker_scores.name: Akshat
speaker_scores.summary: Engaged clearly, articulated business pain points well, and showed genuine enthusiasm for the proposed solution.
strengths: Asked good clarifying questions on tech and timeline
improvements: Could have shared more about internal stakeholders and approval process

risks: Decision-makers on client side not identified — proposal may get stuck without stakeholder buy-in.
No specific follow-up date confirmed — deal momentum may drop after the call.
Audio quality issues could undermine professionalism if repeated on the next call.
Budget range stated without client confirmation — pricing expectations may be misaligned.
